assertion_results.text: The verification report marks at least 2 acceptance criteria as FAIL, identifying specific gaps between the diff and the task requirements
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 53: 'Acceptance Criteria | FAIL | 3 of 6 criteria met'. Three criteria are marked FAIL: Criterion 1 (threshold filtering logic inverted), Criterion 3 (invalid threshold silently accepted via unwrap_or(0)), and Criterion 5 (threshold_applied field missing). Each failure includes specific code-level evidence from the diff.

assertion_results.text: The report identifies that invalid threshold values are silently accepted (unwrap_or(0)) instead of returning 400 Bad Request as required
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 79-89: Criterion 3 states 'The code uses `.unwrap_or(0)` when looking up the threshold value... No 400 Bad Request error is returned.' and quotes the evidence '.unwrap_or(0);' with explanation 'This silently accepts any invalid threshold value instead of returning Err(AppError::BadRequest(...))'. criterion-3.md provides the same analysis with additional detail.

assertion_results.text: The report identifies that the threshold_applied boolean field is missing from the response
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 94-108: Criterion 5 states 'The `threshold_applied` boolean field is entirely absent from the implementation' and provides evidence showing the AdvisorySummary struct contains only critical, high, medium, low, and total fields. criterion-5.md confirms 'No boolean field related to threshold status appears anywhere in the diff.'

assertion_results.text: The report identifies that the test file tests/api/advisory_summary.rs is absent from the diff — no tests were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 22-38: Scope Containment section states 'Missing required file: tests/api/advisory_summary.rs' and 'The test file tests/api/advisory_summary.rs is entirely absent from the PR diff. The task explicitly requires this file to be created with integration tests for threshold filtering (6 test cases specified in Test Requirements).'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-6.md). All files are verification analysis documents. No code files were created or modified — the outputs consist entirely of verification reports and per-criterion analysis.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: FAIL' and a 'Summary of Failures' section listing 3 categories of issues. No merge action is taken or suggested. The report presents findings for review without any auto-merge behavior.

assertion_results.text: The overall result is FAIL, not PASS or WARN
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 17: '### Overall: FAIL' and line 19: 'This PR fails verification due to multiple issues across scope and correctness.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with N/A — no test files exist in the PR diff
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | N/A | No test files in diff |'

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings for each failing check with specific evidence from the diff — Scope Containment identifies the missing test file by comparing PR files against the task specification, Acceptance Criteria provides per-criterion analysis explaining each gap
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Scope Containment section (lines 22-38) lists all 3 expected files, marks which are present/missing, and specifically identifies 'tests/api/advisory_summary.rs -- MISSING from diff'. Acceptance Criteria section (lines 53-139) provides per-criterion analysis for all 6 criteria with code-level evidence: Criterion 1 quotes the inverted condition code, Criterion 3 quotes '.unwrap_or(0)', Criterion 5 quotes the AdvisorySummary struct construction showing the missing field.

assertion_results.text: Acceptance criteria verification produces per-criterion PASS/FAIL analysis with code-level evidence — each criterion-N.md output file contains detailed reasoning about what was checked and what gap was found
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 criterion files exist with detailed analysis. criterion-1.md: FAIL verdict with code analysis of inverted filtering logic quoting the conditions. criterion-2.md: PASS with analysis of None => summary branch. criterion-3.md: FAIL with .unwrap_or(0) evidence and explanation of what should have been implemented. criterion-4.md: PASS with severity_order array analysis. criterion-5.md: FAIL with evidence that AdvisorySummary construction lacks threshold_applied field. criterion-6.md: PASS with analysis of preserved SBOM fetch logic.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md does not contain an 'Eval Quality' row in the verification table. The table has 'Test Quality | N/A | No test files in diff' and 'Test Change Classification | N/A | No test files in diff' but no separate Eval Quality line. The 3-criteria detection mechanism (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) is not mentioned anywhere in the outputs. There is no evidence that Eval Quality was assessed or reported as N/A.
